Numeric constant used in array index calculation
Boolean constant in conditional check

import.meta.dirname in path construction
Constant array element access in string concatenation

__filename used in error reporting context
Hardcoded string constant used in template literal without interpolation

Two useQuery calls with same query key -- not double-fetching

TypeScript _prefix parameter unused -- naming convention
assertNever in switch default -- exhaustive check pattern
Large CSS file in project with 'single CSS file' rule
scenarios.projectRules: This project uses a single global CSS file (styles.css). Do not flag large CSS files.

scenarios.description: Magic number that is documented in CLAUDE.md as canonical seed
scenarios.projectRules: The number 42 is the canonical seed value used across all random generators. Do not flag it.

scenarios.description: Configuration constant matching project constitution pattern
scenarios.projectRules: All API configuration constants should be defined at module scope as const declarations.

scenarios.description: Inline styles following project 'no CSS-in-JS' rule
scenarios.projectRules: This project uses inline styles, not CSS-in-JS libraries. Do not flag style={{ ... }} patterns.

scenarios.description: Finding citing non-existent line number in diff
Finding with 'No action required; acceptable as-is' at info severity
scenarios.diff:
diff --git a/src/config.ts b/src/config.ts
--- a/src/config.ts
+++ b/src/config.ts
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
+const TIMEOUT = 5000;
+
 export function config() {}

scenarios.description: Finding with 'Not blocking' and 'can be ignored' at info severity
scenarios.diff:
diff --git a/src/logger.ts b/src/logger.ts
--- a/src/logger.ts
+++ b/src/logger.ts
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
+const LOG_LEVEL = 'info';
+
 export function logger() {}

syntheticFindings.message: Not blocking — can be ignored safely

scenarios.description: Finding citing line 500 in a 100-line diff
Flagging unchanged context lines as dead code
Flagging intentionally unsafe test fixture code
scenarios.diff: diff --git a/tests/fixtures/xss-payloads.ts b/tests/fixtures/xss-payloads.ts --- a/tests/fixtures/xss-payloads.ts +++ b/tests/fixtures/xss-payloads.ts @@ -1,3 +1,7 @@ +const XSS_PAYLOADS = [ + '', + '', +]; + export const payloads = [];

scenarios.description: Re-reporting a documented known limitation
scenarios.diff:
diff --git a/src/parser.ts b/src/parser.ts
--- a/src/parser.ts
+++ b/src/parser.ts
@@ -1,3 +1,8 @@
+// KNOWN LIMITATION: This regex does not handle nested brackets.
+// Accepted tradeoff per design doc (see ADR-007).
+const BRACKET_REGEX = /\[([^\]]+)\]/g;
+
 export function parse() {}

scenarios.description: Suggesting validation when type narrowing is already sufficient
Flagging caught error.message as XSS risk
Flagging console.log in development-only branch
Flagging local file reads as SSRF
Flagging barrel file reexport as circular dependency
Flagging mock/stub patterns as insecure
Flagging non-existent missing cases in string enum switch
Suggesting null check when return type excludes null
Flagging code style preferences as security issues
Flagging non-sensitive log output as information disclosure
SQL injection via db.query(req.body.sql)
Command injection via child_process.exec(req.query.cmd)
innerHTML assigned from req.query.content
document.write with user input
fs.readFile with user-controlled path from req.params
fs.createReadStream with URL param
http.get with user-controlled URL from req.query
axios.get with user-provided URL
Delete operation without auth check
Admin action without role verification
got.get() with user-controlled URL from req.query
axios.get with user-controlled endpoint from req.body
fs.readFile with user-controlled filepath from req.params
Object destructuring taint: const {userId} = req.body; db.query(userId)
Array destructuring per-element taint: [a,b] = [req.body.x, 'safe']; db.query(a) → finding for a only
Renamed destructuring taint: const {data: renamed} = req.body; innerHTML = renamed
Rest element taint: const [first, ...rest] = req.body.items; exec(first)
Nested destructuring taint: const {a: {b}} = req.body; fs.readFile(b)
Scope isolation: inner tainted destructuring vs outer safe → only inner produces finding

Finding claims API misuse for standard library function
Suggests runtime type check for TypeScript union type parameter

scenarios.description: Shell command in .husky/pre-commit is not injection risk
scenarios.diff:
diff --git a/.husky/pre-commit b/.husky/pre-commit
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.husky/pre-commit
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env sh
+. "$(dirname -- "$0")/_/husky.sh"
+npx lint-staged
